title: Oscar Best Picture Race Analysis: One Battle After Another vs. Sinners
text: This year's Oscar race centers on two films competing for Best Picture supremacy. According to TheWrap's Awards Tracker data, One Battle After Another holds a commanding 99% probability of winning, while Sinners sits at approximately 7.69%. The frontrunner has dominated the major awards circuit: won at BAFTA, PGA, Golden Globes, and Critics Choice Awards; secured the DGA Award which predicts the Oscar winner about 81% of the time this century; and led across multiple pre-Oscar ceremonies. Director Paul Thomas Anderson brings significant credentials, having earned 11 prior nominations without a win, a potential sympathy factor. Despite trailing in traditional metrics, Sinners generated a significant momentum shift by winning the Screen Actors Guild Award for Best Ensemble. The article notes that when a nomination leader also won Best Ensemble at the Actor Awards, it also won Best Picture 89% of the time. However, only two films Crash and Parasite have won Best Picture after winning primarily SAG ensemble recognition, suggesting this path represents a historical anomaly rather than precedent. The analysis predicts One Battle After Another will prevail, though the author acknowledges genuine uncertainty unusual for awards season forecasting.
